question: How many Indians are there, and where is the population heading?

theme.label: People and demography
readerQuestions: Is the trend rising, falling, or slowing?
What is the age or sex structure behind the total?
What does this change for jobs, schools, care, and families?

visualPlan.title: Population, total
visualPlan.recommendedVisualization: single-series line chart with latest value and first-to-latest change
visualPlan.mobileGuidance: Use a line chart with large labels, sparse ticks, and a short reader caption.
visualPlan.whyThisMatters: This is the chart that answers the page question most directly.

plannedCharts.title: India's population, 1960 to today
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The headline count, and the shape of how it got here.

plannedCharts.title: India, China and the world
plannedCharts.subtitle: World Bank · total population · 1990 to today
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: India is now the most populous country on earth, having passed China.

plannedCharts.title: India's share of world population
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: How much of humanity is Indian.

plannedCharts.title: Where Indians live: the slow shift to cities
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Population is also about where people live, and India is urbanising slowly.

plannedCharts.title: Annual population growth rate, to 2100
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: How fast the population changes each year, and when that reaches zero.

plannedCharts.title: Births per 1,000 people
plannedCharts.subtitle: SRS 2024 · crude birth rate · 2019 to 2024
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The flow of new births, India's own latest count.

plannedCharts.title: Births per woman, 1960 to today
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The long arc of fertility, the force behind everything else on this page.

plannedCharts.title: Fertility is now below replacement
plannedCharts.subtitle: SRS 2024 · total fertility rate · 2019 to 2024
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: India's own current reading: national fertility is below the 2.1 replacement level.

plannedCharts.title: Fertility by state
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Fertility is not one number; it is a north-south map.

plannedCharts.title: Highest and lowest fertility states
plannedCharts.subtitle: NFHS-6 (2023-24) · top 5 and bottom 5 of 33 surveyed states/UTs
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The map, sharpened into a league table.

plannedCharts.title: At what age Indian women have children
plannedCharts.subtitle: SRS 2024 · age-specific fertility rate
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Fertility is not just how many, but when.

plannedCharts.title: Women are having children later
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Are women delaying childbirth? The trend says yes.

plannedCharts.title: How India avoids pregnancy, and who carries it
plannedCharts.subtitle: NFHS-6 (2023-24)
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Falling fertility runs on contraception, and in India that burden is overwhelmingly female.

plannedCharts.title: Families are getting smaller
plannedCharts.subtitle: SRS 2024 · live births by birth order
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Smaller families are the texture of below-replacement fertility.

plannedCharts.title: Marrying and giving birth young
plannedCharts.subtitle: NFHS-6 (2023-24)
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Early marriage and teenage motherhood still shape fertility at the margins.

plannedCharts.title: Fertility falls with schooling
plannedCharts.subtitle: NFHS-5 (2019-21) · TFR by years of schooling
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Education is the strongest single lever on fertility.

plannedCharts.title: Fertility falls with wealth
plannedCharts.subtitle: NFHS-5 (2019-21) · TFR by wealth quintile
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Fertility is also a class story.

plannedCharts.title: Fertility by religion
plannedCharts.subtitle: NFHS-5 (2019-21) · TFR by religion
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The most misread fertility number in India, shown plainly.

plannedCharts.title: Boys per girl at birth: the son-preference signal
plannedCharts.subtitle: SRS · sex ratio at birth · 3-year averages
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Fewer children combined with a preference for sons shows up as too few girls born.

plannedCharts.title: India's age pyramid today
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Who those people are, by age and sex.

plannedCharts.title: The age mix is shifting
plannedCharts.subtitle: World Bank · share of population · 1960 to today
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Ageing is three shares moving at once.

plannedCharts.title: Median age, to 2100
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The single number for how old the country is.

plannedCharts.title: Old-age dependency ratio
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: How many older adults each 100 working-age adults support.

plannedCharts.title: Indians are living longer
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The other engine of ageing: longer lives, not just fewer births.

plannedCharts.title: Share of Indians aged 65 and over
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The clearest sign of ageing.

plannedCharts.title: Income per person as India ages
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The 'old before rich' question: how wealthy India is as it greys.

plannedCharts.title: India's population to 2100: the peak
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The payoff: when India stops growing and starts to shrink.

plannedCharts.title: How high, how soon? Scenarios to 2100
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: How much the future hinges on fertility.

plannedCharts.title: The models disagree on the peak
plannedCharts.subtitle: UN WPP 2024 vs IHME (Vollset 2020)
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: How confident can we be about the peak? Compare the two most-cited models.

selectionRules: Start from the page question, not from the indicator name.
Choose the smallest set of visuals that explains scale, trend, structure, comparison, and caveat.
Prefer mobile-friendly charts: line charts with sparse ticks, horizontal bars, share strips, pyramids only when essential, and no dense tables.
Use one strong pull quote only when the evidence supports it with a number.
Add glossary blocks for technical terms that normal readers may not know.
caveats: Use only figures present in lockedNumbers or sourceSummaries.
If a related theme indicator is present, you may use it for context, but do not pretend it is the main answer.
If source coverage is thin, say so directly.
Do not compare states, districts, or cities unless the evidence packet contains those geographies.
forbiddenClaims: Do not infer causality from a trend unless the evidence explicitly supports it.
Do not cite a source that is not present in sourceSummaries.
